Publish.LongDescription:
# Platter: Custom Zoning Overhaul
**Break free from the grid.**
Platter completely reworks zoning in Cities: Skylines 2. It sits right between vanilla zoning and manual building plopping, giving you the control to create realistic neighborhoods, fill awkward gaps, and zone perfectly on curved roads without fighting the grid.
### Key Features * **Manual Zoning:** Place "Parcels" exactly where you want them. No more relying on the grid. * **Precision Snapping:** Includes fully custom snapping options to align parcels perfectly and create exact layouts. * **Custom Setbacks:** Place zones back from the road to create front yards or widen roads later without demolition. * **Curved Road Support:** Finally zone seamlessly along curves. * **Vanilla Simulation:** Parcels use the game's native logic for zoning. Buildings spawn based on actual demand, land value, and desirability.
### How to Use 1. Open the **Platter** tab from the zoning toolbar (or press `Ctrl+P`). 2. Select a parcel size and, optionally, your desired zone type. 3. Click to place the parcel manually — anywhere, at any angle.
### Alpha/Experimental Warning **Platter is currently in ALPHA.** This mod is experimental. While it is considered stable for testing, unexpected issues may occur. * **Backup your save** before using this mod. * Platter can be safely removed from a save by using the "Uninstall" button in the Mod Settings page.
### Support and Troubleshooting If you encounter issues, please head to the [Discord server](https://discord.gg/C2XQUYHwuV) for support. * Please use **Skyve** to share your logs and playset. This is essential for troubleshooting. Sharing your save is also extremely helpful. * Bug reports without logs are difficult to fix!
### Compatibility * Fully compatible with major mods such as **Move It**, **Zone Reorganizer**, **Advanced Line Tool**, etc.
### Known Issues and FAQ * **Grid Overrides:** The vanilla grid may sometimes override the Parcel grid. Investigation and fix is underway. * **Snapping Quirks:** While custom snapping is included, snapping may sometimes act in unexpected ways depending on the angle. Improvements are planned.
### Translations I am looking for volunteers to help translate the mod into other languages. * If you are interested, please join the [Discord server](https://discord.gg/C2XQUYHwuV) to collaborate with other translators. * You can also contribute directly via [CrowdIn](https://crowdin.com/project/platter-cs2).
### Donations Donations are incredibly kind and appreciated. If you'd like to show appreciation and help me continue developing mods, the link to Ko-fi is in the sidebar!
### Credits **Creator** * Luca
**Translators** * Nullpinter * elGendo87 * BartqeM * Morgan Touverey * MarKing * SZyMeX * GuysLetsPlay * to630 * smile1509 * redlabracer * AngelicaVee
**Special Thanks** * The modding community on Discord for testing and feedback. * DanielVNZ, saladuit, astrognash, MarKing, Honu, and countless others for testing the mod and putting up with my changes. * krzychu124, yenyang, algernon, KJ, and many others for help with code, modding, management, and other topics.
Publish.ChangeLog:
# Version 1.5.1 (Same as 1.5.0, due to a publishing error)
**New parcel sizes** - Added Narrow (1 cell wide) parcels - Added Extra-Wide (7,8 cells wide) parcels
**Snapping improvements** - Revamped snapping and added snap support for start and end of line tool - Refactored the way snapping injects itself into the base game - Improved logic and tweaked parameters for various snapping options, especially when multiple options are used together. - Improved the way platter let's the base game know a snap has occurred, which for example should remove the "rotation" options from view when snapped.
**Notable Bug Fixes** - Fixed: Unzoned parcel cells would be "eaten" by the vanilla grid in some cases - Fixed: Game would crash when drawing roads in some cases - Fixed: Parcels would connect to unzoned RoadBuilder roads, causing issues - Fixed: Relocate and Picker would not work correctly when used with Parcels - Fixed: Parcels would cause some buildings to lose props and trees when growing - Fixed: Zone indexes would get out of sync when adding or removing zones between save loads, causing incorrect parcel colors and prezoning behavior
**Other notable improvements** - Improved the building count tooltip accuracy and clarity - Adds keyboard-based controls for parcel sizes - Adjusted look and feel of the parcel overlay - More reliably detect corner lots when placing parcels - Improve handling of vanilla bulldoze and select - Significantly improved performance of the parcel overlay - Improved performance when caching and delivering data to the UI, especially for the pre-zone panel - Improved the way the road connection icon is added and removed - Improved performance of internal prefab selection mechanisms by caching references to important prefabs in memory - Numerous performance improvements to core systems/queries - Updated translations - Added a colored outline to unzoned Platter cells to distinguish them from vanilla unzoned cells - Fixed version mismatch between PDXMods version and binary version, which caused some confusion and issues with mod updates.
